page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.2xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family, utilizing the ARMv9 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a Neoverse-V2 core design. The CPU configuration provides 8 vCPUs with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is not active. The processor is equipped with 64 KB of L1D cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is provisioned with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gb.2xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, highlighted by a top-tier PassMark memory score and strong large-block memory write bandwidth of 86.29 GB/sec. Single-threaded operations perform well, with strong results in Geekbench single-core tests, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. Multi-threaded and raw computational tasks, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core tests, fall into the average range. Cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, with the PassMark CPU encryption test registering in the bottom 25%. For specialized workloads, the server handles small-model LLM inference at average speeds, though prompt processing for medium 7B models is poor.

The m8gb.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile, combining dedicated ARMv9 cores with high memory bandwidth. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that benefit from high memory throughput and single-threaded efficiency without requiring local storage. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations such as Redis caching.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.2xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier memory performance and strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and compression, though cryptographic performance is relatively weak. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory bandwidth and dedicated CPU resources. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.2xlarge is an ARMv9-based general-purpose server featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB of RAM, and 17 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.2xlarge is an ARMv9 general-purpose server with 8 dedicated cores, 32 GB RAM, and 17 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: An ARMv9-powered general-purpose server delivering dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for balanced, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading
32.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier memory bandwidth
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-core performance in compression and text processing
Optimized for general-purpose applications, web serving, and databases
